1292→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1293→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1294→
1295→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1296→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1297→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1298→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1299→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1300→        }
1301→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1302→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1303→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1304→            }
1305→        })
1306→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1307→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1308→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1309→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1310→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1311→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1312→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1313→                    )
1314→                )
1315→            ) {
1316→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1317→                v.performClick()
1318→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1319→            }
1320→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1321→        }
1322→    }

1359→    /**

1361→     * @return true if there is another card of same note that could be dismissed
1362→     */
1363→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1364→    private fun suspendNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1365→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1366→            false
1367→        } else {
1368→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1369→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1370→                currentCard!!.nid,
1371→                currentCard!!.id
1372→            ) == 1
1373→        }
1374→        // whether there exists a sibling not buried.
1375→    }
1376→
1377→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1378→    private fun buryNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1379→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1380→            false
1381→        } else {
1382→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1383→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue >=  " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_NEW + " limit 1",
1384→                currentCard!!.nid,
1385→                currentCard!!.id
1386→            ) == 1
1387→        }
1388→        // Whether there exists a sibling which is neither suspended nor buried
1389→    }

 39→object CollectionManager {
 40→    /**
 41→     * The currently active backend, which is created on demand via [ensureBackend], and
 42→     * implicitly via [ensureOpen] and routines like [withCol].
 43→     * The backend is long-lived, and will generally only be closed when switching interface
 44→     * languages or changing schema versions. A closed backend cannot be reused, and a new one
 45→     * must be created.
 46→     */
 47→    private var backend: Backend? = null
 48→
 49→    /**
 50→     * The current collection, which is opened on demand via [withCol]. If you need to
 51→     * close and reopen the collection in an atomic operation, add a new method that
 52→     * calls [withQueue], and then executes [ensureClosedInner] and [ensureOpenInner] inside it.
 53→     * A closed collection can be detected via [withOpenColOrNull] or by checking [Collection.dbClosed].
 54→     */
 55→    private var collection: Collection? = null
 56→
 57→    private var queue: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO.limitedParallelism(1)
 58→
 59→    @VisibleForTesting
 60→    var emulateOpenFailure = false
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * Execute the provided block on a serial background queue, to ensure
 64→     * concurrent access does not happen.
 65→     *
 66→     * The background queue is run in a [Dispatchers.IO] context.
 67→     * It's important that the block is not suspendable - if it were, it would allow
 68→     * multiple requests to be interleaved when a suspend point was hit.
 69→     *
 70→     * TODO Disallow running functions that are supposed to be run inside the queue outside of it.
 71→     *   For instance, this can be done by marking a [block] with a context
 72→     *   that cannot be instantiated outside of this class:
 73→     *
 74→     *       suspend fun<T> withQueue(block: context(Queue) () -> T): T {
 75→     *          return withContext(collectionOperationsDispatcher) {
 76→     *              block(queue)
 77→     *          }
 78→     *      }
 79→     *
 80→     *   Then, only functions that are also marked can be run inside the block:
 81→     *
 82→     *       context(Queue) suspend fun canOnlyBeRunInWithQueue()
 83→     */
 84→    private suspend fun<T> withQueue(@WorkerThread block: CollectionManager.() -> T): T {
 85→        return withContext(queue) {
 86→            this@CollectionManager.block()
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Execute the provided block with the collection, opening if necessary.
 92→     *
 93→     * Calls are serialized, and run in background [Dispatchers.IO] thread.
 94→     *
 95→     * Parallel calls to this function are guaranteed to be serialized, so you can be
 96→     * sure the collection won't be closed or modified by another thread. This guarantee
 97→     * does not hold if legacy code calls [getColUnsafe].
 98→     */
 99→    suspend fun <T> withCol(@WorkerThread block: Collection.() -> T): T {
100→        return withQueue {
101→            ensureOpenInner()
102→            block(collection!!)
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    /**
107→     * Execute the provided block if the collection is already open. See [withCol] for more.
108→     * Since the block may return a null value, and a null value will also be returned in the
109→     * case of the collection being closed, if the calling code needs to distinguish between
110→     * these two cases, it should wrap the return value of the block in a class (eg Optional),
111→     * instead of returning a nullable object.
112→     */
113→    suspend fun<T> withOpenColOrNull(@WorkerThread block: Collection.() -> T): T? {
114→        return withQueue {
115→            if (collection != null && !collection!!.dbClosed) {
116→                block(collection!!)
117→            } else {
118→                null
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    /**
124→     * Return a handle to the backend, creating if necessary. This should only be used
125→     * for routines that don't depend on an open or closed collection, such as checking
126→     * the current progress state when importing a colpkg file. While the backend is
127→     * thread safe and can be accessed concurrently, if another thread closes the collection
128→     * and you call a routine that expects an open collection, it will result in an error.
129→     */
130→    fun getBackend(): Backend {
131→        if (backend == null) {
132→            runBlocking { withQueue { ensureBackendInner() } }
133→        }
134→        return backend!!
135→    }
136→
137→    /**
138→     * Translations provided by the Rust backend/Anki desktop code.
139→     */
140→    val TR: Translations
141→        get() {
142→            // we bypass the lock here so that translations are fast - conflicts are unlikely,
143→            // as the backend is only ever changed on language preference change or schema switch
144→            return getBackend().tr
145→        }
146→
147→    fun compareAnswer(expected: String, given: String): String {
148→        // bypass the lock, as the type answer code is heavily nested in non-suspend funs
149→        return getBackend().compareAnswer(expected, given)
150→    }
151→
152→    /**
153→     * Close the currently cached backend and discard it. Useful when enabling the V16 scheduler in the
154→     * dev preferences, or if the active language changes. Saves and closes the collection if open.
155→     */
156→    suspend fun discardBackend() {
157→        withQueue {
158→            discardBackendInner()
159→        }
160→    }
161→
162→    /** See [discardBackend]. This must only be run inside the queue. */
163→    private fun discardBackendInner() {
164→        ensureClosedInner()
165→        if (backend != null) {
166→            backend!!.close()
167→            backend = null
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * Open the backend if it's not already open.
173→     */
174→    private suspend fun ensureBackend() {
175→        withQueue {
176→            ensureBackendInner()
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    /** See [ensureBackend]. This must only be run inside the queue. */
181→    private fun ensureBackendInner() {
182→        if (backend == null) {
183→            backend = BackendFactory.getBackend()
184→        }
185→    }
186→
187→    /**
188→     * If the collection is open, close it.
189→     */
190→    suspend fun ensureClosed() {
191→        withQueue {
192→            ensureClosedInner()
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    /** See [ensureClosed]. This must only be run inside the queue. */
197→    private fun ensureClosedInner() {
198→        if (collection == null) {
199→            return
200→        }
201→        try {
202→            collection!!.close()
203→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
204→            Timber.e("swallowing error on close: $exc")
205→        }
206→        collection = null
207→    }
208→
209→    /**
210→     * Open the collection, if it's not already open.
211→     *
212→     * Automatically called by [withCol]. Can be called directly to ensure collection
213→     * is loaded at a certain point in time, or to ensure no errors occur.
214→     */
215→    private suspend fun ensureOpen() {
216→        withQueue {
217→            ensureOpenInner()
218→        }
219→    }

 82→    override fun onDraw(canvas: Canvas) {

 91→    /** Handle motion events to draw using the touch screen or to interact with the flashcard behind
 92→     * the whiteboard by using a second finger.
 93→     *
 94→     * @param event The motion event.
 95→     * @return True if the event was handled, false otherwise
 96→     */
 97→    fun handleTouchEvent(event: MotionEvent): Boolean {
 98→        return handleDrawEvent(event) || handleMultiTouchEvent(event)
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Handle motion events to draw using the touch screen. Only simple touch events are processed,
103→     * a multitouch event aborts to current stroke.
104→     *
105→     * @param event The motion event.
106→     * @return True if the event was handled, false otherwise or when drawing was aborted due to
107→     * detection of a multitouch event.
108→     */
109→    private fun handleDrawEvent(event: MotionEvent): Boolean {
110→        val x = event.x
111→        val y = event.y
112→        if (event.getToolType(event.actionIndex) == MotionEvent.TOOL_TYPE_ERASER) {
113→            stylusErase(event)
114→            return true
115→        }
116→        if (event.getToolType(event.actionIndex) != MotionEvent.TOOL_TYPE_STYLUS && toggleStylus) {
117→            return false
118→        }
119→        return when (event.actionMasked) {
120→            MotionEvent.ACTION_DOWN -> {
121→                drawStart(x, y)
122→                invalidate()
123→                true
124→            }
125→            MotionEvent.ACTION_MOVE -> {
126→                if (isCurrentlyDrawing) {
127→                    for (i in 0 until event.historySize) {
128→                        drawAlong(event.getHistoricalX(i), event.getHistoricalY(i))
129→                    }
130→                    drawAlong(x, y)
131→                    invalidate()
132→                    return true
133→                }
134→                false
135→            }
136→            MotionEvent.ACTION_UP -> {
137→                if (isCurrentlyDrawing) {
138→                    drawFinish()
139→                    invalidate()
140→                    return true
141→                }
142→                false
143→            }
144→            MotionEvent.ACTION_POINTER_DOWN -> {
145→                if (isCurrentlyDrawing) {
146→                    drawAbort()
147→                }
148→                false
149→            }
150→            211, 213 -> {
151→                if (event.buttonState == MotionEvent.BUTTON_STYLUS_PRIMARY) {
152→                    stylusErase(event)
153→                }
154→                true
155→            }
156→            else -> false
157→        }
158→    }
159→
160→    // Parse multitouch input to scroll the card behind the whiteboard or click on elements
161→    private fun handleMultiTouchEvent(event: MotionEvent): Boolean {
162→        return if (mHandleMultiTouch && event.pointerCount == 2) {
163→            when (event.actionMasked) {
164→                MotionEvent.ACTION_POINTER_DOWN -> {
165→                    reinitializeSecondFinger(event)
166→                    true
167→                }
168→                MotionEvent.ACTION_MOVE -> trySecondFingerScroll(event)
169→                MotionEvent.ACTION_POINTER_UP -> trySecondFingerClick(event)
170→                else -> false
171→            }
172→        } else {
173→            false
174→        }
175→    }
176→
177→    /**
178→     * Erase with stylus pen.(By using the eraser button on the stylus pen or by using the digital eraser)
179→     */
180→    private fun stylusErase(event: MotionEvent) {
181→        if (!undoEmpty()) {
182→            val didErase = mUndo.erase(event.x.toInt(), event.y.toInt())
183→            if (didErase) {
184→                mUndo.apply()
185→                if (undoEmpty()) {
186→                    mAnkiActivity.invalidateOptionsMenu()
187→                }
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    /**
193→     * Clear the whiteboard.
194→     */
195→    fun clear() {
196→        mBitmap.eraseColor(0)
197→        mUndo.clear()
198→        invalidate()
199→        mAnkiActivity.invalidateOptionsMenu()
200→    }
201→
202→    /**
203→     * Undo the last stroke
204→     */
205→    fun undo() {
206→        mUndo.pop()
207→        mUndo.apply()
208→        if (undoEmpty()) {
209→            mAnkiActivity.invalidateOptionsMenu()
210→        }
211→    }
212→
213→    /** @return Whether there are strokes to undo
214→     */
215→    fun undoEmpty(): Boolean {
216→        return mUndo.empty()
217→    }

220→        val bitmap = Bitmap.createBitmap(w, h, Bitmap.Config.ARGB_8888)
221→        mBitmap = bitmap

 86→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 87→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->

 96→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {

 98→            // do not reorder the elements
 99→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
100→                return@BiFunction false
101→            }
102→            collection.add(element)
103→            return@BiFunction true
104→        }
105→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
106→    }

111→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
112→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
113→    }

 17→/**
 18→ * With the Rust backend, operations that modify the collection return a description of changes (OpChanges).
 19→ * The UI can subscribe to these changes, so it can update itself when actions have been performed
 20→ * (eg, the deck list can check if studyQueues has been updated, and if so, it will redraw the list).
 21→ *
 22→ * The optional handler argument can be used so that the initiator of an action can tell when a
 23→ * OpChanges action was caused by itself. This can be useful when the default change behaviour
 24→ * should be ignored, in favour of specific handling (eg the UI wishes to directly update the
 25→ * displayed flag, without redrawing the entire review screen).
 26→ */
 27→
 28→package com.ichi2.libanki
 29→
 30→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 31→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 32→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 33→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 34→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 35→import anki.import_export.ImportResponse
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
 37→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 38→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 39→import java.lang.ref.WeakReference
 40→
 41→object ChangeManager {
 42→    interface Subscriber {
 43→        /**
 44→         * Called after a backend method invoked via col.op() or col.opWithProgress()
 45→         * has modified the collection. Subscriber should inspect the changes, and update
 46→         * the UI if necessary.
 47→         */
 48→        fun opExecuted(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?)
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private val subscribers = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 52→
 53→    fun subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber) {
 54→        subscribers.add(WeakReference(subscriber))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun notifySubscribers(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?) {
 58→        val expired = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 59→        for (subscriber in subscribers) {
 60→            val ref = subscriber.get()
 61→            if (ref == null) {
 62→                expired.add(subscriber)
 63→            } else {
 64→                ref.opExecuted(changes, handler)
 65→            }
 66→        }
 67→        for (item in expired) {
 68→            subscribers.remove(item)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    @VisibleForTesting
 73→    fun clearSubscribers() {
 74→        subscribers.clear()
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    internal fun <T> notifySubscribers(changes: T, initiator: Any?) {
 78→        val opChanges = when (changes) {
 79→            is OpChanges -> changes
 80→            is OpChangesWithCount -> changes.changes
 81→            is OpChangesWithId -> changes.changes
 82→            is OpChangesAfterUndo -> changes.changes
 83→            is ImportResponse -> changes.changes
 84→            else -> TODO("unhandled change type")
 85→        }
 86→        notifySubscribers(opChanges, initiator)
 87→    }
 88→}
 89→
 90→/** Wrap a routine that returns OpChanges* or similar undo info with this
 91→ * to notify change subscribers of the changes. */
 92→suspend fun <T> undoableOp(handler: Any? = null, block: Collection.() -> T): T {
 93→    return withCol {
 94→        block()
 95→    }.also {
 96→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
 97→            ChangeManager.notifySubscribers(it, handler)
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→}
